issues.description: The social proof section uses an <h3> ('Ils nous font confiance') as the first heading after the H1 hero, with no H2 between them. This skips a heading level and breaks the document outline, which affects both accessibility and SEO.

The Font Awesome Duotone icon span is rendered as an empty box (no icon character in the HTML content), leaving a visible blank ~37px square before every FAQ question. The icon ligature/glyph is missing from the markup, resulting in a broken visual layout.

The nav 'Demander une démo' button links to '/fr/meetings-pages', while the hero primary CTA 'Réservez une démo' also links to '/fr/meetings-pages'. These are consistent, but the nav label uses 'Demander' while the hero uses 'Réservez' — minor inconsistency aside, the real issue is that the secondary hero CTA '▶️ Découvrir l'outil PMO en vidéo (5 min)' links to '/fr/video/pmo', which should be verified as a real route and not a stub.

The subtitle refers to 'celui qui fait les PowerPoints' and 'celui qui pilote la transformation' using masculine singular forms, which excludes feminine-identifying PMO professionals. On a B2B landing page targeting a diverse audience this may feel exclusionary, and at minimum warrants review.

Two consecutive feature sections ('KPI : Taux d'avancement du Quarter Plan' and 'Planification trimestrielle structurée') both show a Quarter Plan screenshot from a similar angle with very similar framing, making them look like duplicates at a glance. The images should be differentiated.

Section heading is 'Flash Report en 1 clic' — 'Flash Report' is an acceptable AirSaas brand term, but the body copy says 'aux couleurs de votre entreprise. Le Comex est informé' which introduces 'Comex' (a French corporate term) while the hero and other sections reference 'Codir'. Using both terms interchangeably on the same page may confuse visitors about which governance body is targeted.

The answer to the pricing question is 'Prix accessible. Parlons-en lors d'une démo.' — this is a non-answer that deflects rather than informs. On a landing page, this reads as evasive and may erode trust; at minimum it should give a pricing range or link to a pricing page.

The logo strip section uses an H3 ('Ils nous font confiance') where an H2 is semantically expected — the page jumps from H1 (hero) directly to H3, skipping H2 entirely in the document outline at this point, breaking heading hierarchy.

This is fine, but the nav label 'Demander une démo' should be verified to resolve correctly; the path 'meetings-pages' (English slug in a French route) looks like a leftover stub that may 404.

issues.location: Trusted logos section — all logo images
issues.description: All five client logo elements have empty alt attributes (alt=""). While decorative images can use empty alt, trust-logo sections conventionally name the company for accessibility and context; anonymous logos provide no fallback if images fail to load.

The secondary CTA '📘 Découvrir le guide Capacity Planning' uses a book emoji prefix which is unconventional for a branded design system button and may render inconsistently across OS/browsers. The emoji also does not match the primary palette style of other buttons on the page.

The FAQ H2 splits 'Questions' and 'fréquentes' into two separate gradient spans with different gradient directions, causing the two words to have visually mismatched colours that may look unintentional (dark-to-primary vs. primary). This is the only heading on the page with a split two-gradient treatment.

issues.location: Trusted logos section / 'Ils nous font confiance'

The 'Ils nous font confiance' band and all subsequent feature sections also use H3, meaning there is no H2 between the H1 and any of these headings — the heading hierarchy skips H2 entirely for the main content sections, which breaks document outline and SEO for a landing page.

Each FAQ button contains a Font Awesome Duotone icon rendered via a CSS font-family span, but the span has no text content — it is visually empty. The icon will not render in any browser that does not have Font Awesome 6 Duotone loaded, leaving a blank 2.3rem-wide gap to the left of every question label.

The primary CTA 'Réservez une démo' links to '/fr/meetings-pages' while the nav button also links to '/fr/meetings-pages' with the label 'Demander une démo'. Two buttons on the same hero use inconsistent labels for the same destination, which is acceptable, but the secondary CTA links to '/fr/video/ppm' — this URL pattern should be verified as it is not referenced anywhere else in the visible site structure and may be a dead link.

The price answer 'Prix accessible. Tarification sur mesure selon la taille de votre organisation. Parlons-en lors d'une démo.' is vague to the point of being unhelpful — it contains no actual pricing information or a link to a pricing page. For a PPM landing page targeting enterprise buyers, this is likely to hurt conversion and may be stub/placeholder copy.

Odd-numbered feature sections use 'bg-white' with 'lg:pl-[10rem] lg:pr-0' and even-numbered use 'bg-white' with 'lg:pr-[10rem] lg:pl-0'. Both background colors resolve to white (the even sections use color-prevention-10 for the image container only). With no visual section separator between 6 consecutive white-background feature sections, the page will appear as a single undifferentiated wall of content on desktop — there is no alternating background or divider to visually separate the sections.

The subtitle 'Brief projet assisté par IA, flash report en 1 clic, roadmap partageable, vue macro consolidée.' lists four features but 'brief projet assisté par IA' does not appear as a named feature section on the page (the sections cover Flash Report, Roadmap, Priorisation, Décisions, Portfolio, Capacité, Scénarios). The hero promises an AI brief feature that has no corresponding section, potentially creating a user expectation mismatch.

The copy reads 'AirSaas transforme les décisions en objets pilotables' — within a feature-detail section that is otherwise written in the second person / impersonal voice. Sudden brand self-reference mid-paragraph is stylistically inconsistent with the rest of the page copy and reads like it was imported from a different content block.

All five logo images have empty alt attributes (alt=''), which is acceptable for decorative logos, but the section heading 'Ils nous font confiance' identifies them as meaningful social proof. At minimum the company names should be in the alt text for screen reader users who cannot see which companies trust AirSaas.

Should read something like 'Les outils PPM traditionnels sont souvent complexes… AirSaas propose…'.

issues.location: Section 'Améliorez votre gestion des ressources' — internal link

Mildly confusing French that may mislead readers scanning the hero.

issues.location: Feature section — 'Synchronisez vos outils préférés' — body copy

The hero paragraph contains a visible Webflow zero-width joiner artifact (‍) mid-sentence ('le grand guide de la conduite de projet.‍Mesurer ses performances…'), causing a line break or invisible character that splits the sentence unexpectedly for users.

Seven consecutive sections between the hero and the feature image rows render as raw paragraph/list content with no heading (no H2/H3), no section title, and no visual container differentiation. The sole hero CTA 'Réservez une démo' links to '/fr/meetings-pages', which is the same destination as the nav bar 'Demander une démo' button — acceptable — but the hero section has only one CTA with no secondary option (e.g. product tour or login), which is weaker than the live page and may reduce conversion.
The copyright reads '© 2026 AirSaas' — the year 2026 is in the future and is almost certainly a typo; it should be 2024 or 2025.

This section uses minimal vertical padding (py-[2.5rem] / lg:py-[4rem]) compared to all surrounding feature sections (lg:py-[6.25rem]), making it feel visually cramped and inconsistent with the page rhythm.

The image shown (alt="Kanban") is sourced from "Portfolio%20header%20menu.webp" — a header/menu screenshot — not an actual Kanban board view. The image does not match the section it illustrates, which will confuse visitors.

The figure caption reads 'Airsaas rassemble vos données projets' using 'Airsaas' (lowercase 's') instead of the brand-correct 'AirSaas'. The same casing error appears in the alt text of the same image.
A missing space before 'Gartner' in 'l'institut d'étudesGartner' causes the two words to render concatenated ('étudesGartner'). This is a visible typographic defect in the opening paragraph.

The article body opens directly with an H3 ('PPM : trois innovations qui font la différence') with no H2 above it, then uses H4 sub-sections under it. The hierarchy skips H2 entirely, which is semantically incorrect and produces an illogical document outline.

The caption reads 'Accélération digitale / Transformation et changement sont le quotidient des organisations' — 'quotidient' is not a French word; the intended word is 'quotidien' (typo in the source content carried into the rebuild).
The caption reads 'L'un des meilleurs conseils produigué :-)' — 'produigué' is not a French word (likely a misspelling of 'prodigué'). Minor typo but user-visible.
